equivalence_argument: The HTTP codec implements the schema and header/body validation but uses a different accepted method range than the invariant requires.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: HTTP method validation does not accept method value 0 and does accept method value 7, instead of accepting only values 0 through 6.
related_but_insufficient_code: HTTPCallRequest::from_abi decodes the full ABI layout, pairs headers, rejects header length mismatch, and maps empty body to None.
HTTPMethod::try_from accepts values 1 through 7 and rejects 0, which contradicts the invariant requirement to accept only 0 through 6.

rationale: The commit adds a concrete HTTP precompile request schema and validation logic with unit tests for full decoding, method validation, and header-length mismatch. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: HTTPCallRequest::from_abi must decode the ABI layout [executor, encrypted_secrets, ttl, url, method, header keys, header values, body], accept only HTTP method values 0 through 6, reject mismatched header key/value array lengths, pair headers in order, and represent an empty body as None.
suggested_falsification_tests: ABI-encode a valid HTTP call request and assert all decoded fields match, then encode method 99 and mismatched header arrays and assert InvalidFieldValue and HeaderLengthMismatch respectively; encode an empty body and assert body is None.
summary: HTTP call decoding has the wrong accepted method range for this invariant.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Decode HTTP call payloads with method 0, 6, and 7; current code rejects 0 and accepts 7, contrary to the invariant.
title: HTTP call requests use validated typed ABI decoding
